column1: CX - Configure Forth webhook for customer profile updates in CreditorX
column32:
*Description*
Configure and validate a Forth webhook to notify CreditorX whenever a customer's profile information is updated. The primary goal is to keep the customer's email address synchronized; however, Forth does not provide an event specifically for email changes. Instead, the configured webhook is triggered whenever any customer profile information is modified. The webhook payload has been configured to include the {{ContactID}} and {{Email}}, allowing the application to synchronize the customer's email when applicable.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* -Webhook configured to receive customer profile update events from Forth-
* -Webhook payload includes {{ContactID}} and {{Email}}-

*Deliverable*
The *Form* webhook event was configured and validated, confirming that it is triggered whenever any customer information is updated *through Forth CRM* (such as email, phone number, or name), *not only when the email changes*. The payload was configured to include the {{ContactID}} and {{Email}}, allowing CreditorX to synchronize the customer's email when applicable. It is important to note that Forth also supports customer updates through the *API* and *Data Source* event types, but these webhooks have *not* been configured yet, as they are expected to generate additional notifications for any customer profile change and should be evaluated before being enabled.

* -Ability to switch between environments without code changes-

column1: CP - IOS - Implement localized error handling for iOS
column32:
*Descripción:* 

Error messages returned by the backend include an {{errorKey}} and a fallback {{message}}. iOS currently only displays the raw message, ignoring the key. Errors should be resolved using the app's i18n system to display them in the active language, matching the behavior already implemented in the frontend.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* Error responses include {{errorKey}} and are resolved to a localized string
* Active language (ES/EN) is respected when displaying errors
* Falls back to the backend's default message if no translation exists for the key
* Error args (e.g. {{{minutes}}}) are interpolated in the translated string

*Deliverables*

column1: CP - WEB - Add environment indicator banner
column32:
*Description* 

There is currently no visual way to identify which environment the frontend is pointing to during development and testing. This makes it easy to accidentally test against the wrong backend or ship a build thinking it targets production when it does not. This task adds a persistent visual banner that displays the current environment (Local, Dev, Staging) and is automatically hidden in Production builds.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* -A banner is visible on all screens when running in Local, Dev, or Staging environments-
* -The banner is not rendered in Production builds-
* -The banner displays the current environment name-
* -No code changes are required to switch the banner — it reflects the active environment automatically-

*Deliverables*

column1: CP - IOS - Implement support physical device on Local environment  and centralize backend URLs (Refactor)
column32:
*Description*
Backend URLs were duplicated across {{EndPointsMoya.swift}} and {{URLServicesGQL.swift}}, each maintaining its own {{#if LOCAL / STAGING / DEBUG / else}} switch. Any URL change required updating two files. Additionally, running the Local scheme on a physical device failed because {{localhost}} resolves to the device itself, not the developer's Mac.
This task centralizes all environment base URLs into a single source of truth and enables physical device support for the Local environment using the Mac's mDNS hostname ({{.local}}), while keeping the developer-specific hostname out of the repository.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* -All environment base URLs are defined in a single place — no duplication across files-
* -Switching environments requires no code changes, only selecting the corresponding Xcode scheme-
* -The Local scheme works on both simulator and physical device-
* -Developer-specific configuration is not committed to the repository-

*Deliverables*

[https://github.com/cordoba-tech/cordoba-consumer-portal-ios/pull/18|https://github.com/cordoba-tech/cordoba-consumer-portal-ios/pull/18|smart-link]

column1: CP - IOS - Add environment indicator banner for non-production builds
column32:
*Description*

Currently, there is no visual indicator in the app showing which backend environment is active. This makes it easy to confuse Dev, Staging, and Local builds during testing and QA. This task adds a persistent colored banner that displays the current environment name, visible in all non-production builds.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* -A thin banner is displayed at the bottom of the screen in all non-production builds (Local, Dev, Staging)-
* -The banner label and color reflect the active environment:-

* Local → purple / {{LOCAL}}
* Dev → blue / {{DEV}}
* Staging → orange / {{STAGING}}

* -The banner is completely absent in Production builds — no dead code, no hidden views — excluded at compile time via {{#if !PRODUCTION}}-
* -The banner persists across all screens and navigation transitions-
* -Tapping or interacting with the banner does not block touches to the underlying UI-

*Deliverables*

[https://github.com/cordoba-tech/cordoba-consumer-portal-ios/pull/18|https://github.com/cordoba-tech/cordoba-consumer-portal-ios/pull/18|smart-link]

column32:

Currently, the iOS app has a single default scheme that points to the development backend. Developers must manually change build configurations or hardcode URLs to test against different environments. This task creates dedicated Xcode schemes for each backend environment so developers can switch environments directly from the scheme selector in Xcode.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* -Dedicated shared schemes exist for Local, Dev, Staging, and Production environments-
* -Each scheme sets the correct build configuration, activating the corresponding compilation flag ({{LOCAL}}, {{DEBUG}}, {{STAGING}}, {{PRODUCTION}}) used by {{EndPointsMoya.swift}} to resolve the backend URL-
* -Selecting a scheme and running the app hits the correct backend-

*Deliverables*
